NCT02091375.name: GWPCARE1
NCT02091375.group: Dravet syndrome, cannabidiol 20 mg/kg/d vs placebo (wk 14, >=50% convulsive-seizure reduction RR)

allOutcomes.title: Proportion with >=50% reduction in convulsive seizure frequency (secondary; primary was median % reduction) (RR)

NCT02091375.snippet: Source: Devinsky O et al. NEJM 2017;376:2011-2020 (GWPCARE1 Dravet).

evidence.label: Enrollment
evidence.text: 120 children and young adults (age 2-18) with Dravet syndrome and drug-resistant convulsive seizures (>=4/month during 4-week baseline) randomized 1:1 double-blind to pharmaceutical-grade cannabidiol (Epidiolex) 20 mg/kg/d oral solution in divided doses (n=61) or matched placebo (n=59) over 14 weeks as adjunctive to existing antiepileptic regimen.

evidence.label: Primary - Median % Convulsive Seizure Reduction
evidence.text: Median % change in convulsive seizure frequency from baseline: CBD -38.9% vs placebo -13.3%; estimated median treatment difference -22.8 percentage points (95% CI -41.1 to -5.4; P=0.01). Primary endpoint met.

evidence.label: Secondary - >=50% Responder Rate
evidence.text: Proportion with >=50% reduction in convulsive seizures: CBD 43% (26/61) vs placebo 27% (16/59); odds ratio 2.00 (95% CI 0.93-4.30; NS). Somnolence, decreased appetite, diarrhoea more common on CBD; transaminase elevations (usually with concomitant valproate).

evidence.label: Risk of Bias (RoB 2.0)
evidence.text: D1 LOW - central IVR. D2 LOW - double-blind matched placebo. D3 LOW - 93% completion. D4 LOW - electronic seizure diary, central review. D5 LOW - SAP locked.

evidence.label: Eligibility (AACT-derived)
evidence.text: AACT-derived (auto-populated 2026-05-04). Sex: ALL; min age 2 Years; max age 18 Years; healthy volunteers: f. CRITERIA: "Key Inclusion Criteria:~* Participants were male or female aged between 2 and 18 years (inclusive).~* Participants had a documented history of Dravet Syndrome that was not completely controlled by current antiepileptic drugs.~* Participants took one or more antiepileptic drugs at a dose that had been stable for at least four weeks.~* All medications or interventions for epilepsy (including ketogenic diet and vagus nerve stimulation) were stable for four weeks prior to screening and participants...

NCT02224690.name: GWPCARE2
NCT02224690.group: Dravet syndrome, cannabidiol 20 mg/kg/d vs placebo (wk 14, >=50% responder RR; 20 mg/kg arm)

allOutcomes.title: Proportion with >=50% reduction in convulsive seizure frequency (20 mg/kg arm) (RR)

NCT02224690.snippet: Source: Miller I et al. JAMA Neurol 2020;77:613-621 (GWPCARE2 Dravet).

evidence.label: Enrollment
evidence.text: 198 children and young adults with Dravet syndrome randomized 1:1:1 double-blind to CBD 10 mg/kg/d (n=66), CBD 20 mg/kg/d (n=67), or matched placebo (n=65) for 14 weeks as adjunctive.

evidence.label: Primary - Median % Convulsive Seizure Reduction
evidence.text: Median % change in convulsive seizures: CBD 20 mg/kg -45.7% vs CBD 10 mg/kg -48.7% vs placebo -26.9%. Treatment difference 20 mg/kg vs placebo: -18.8 pp (95% CI -34.5 to -3.1; P=0.02). Primary met.

evidence.label: Secondary - Responder Rate
evidence.text: >=50% responder rate (convulsive seizures): CBD 20 mg/kg 46.3% (31/67) vs placebo 26.2% (17/65). Serious AEs 25% vs 15%. Somnolence 13%, decreased appetite 19%, elevated LFTs 20%.

evidence.label: Risk of Bias (RoB 2.0)
evidence.text: D1 LOW. D2 LOW - double-blind. D3 LOW - 94% completion. D4 LOW. D5 LOW - SAP locked.

evidence.label: Eligibility (AACT-derived)
evidence.text: AACT-derived (auto-populated 2026-05-04). Sex: ALL; min age 2 Years; max age 55 Years; healthy volunteers: f. CRITERIA: "Key Inclusion Criteria:~* Participant must have been male or female aged between 2 and 55 years (inclusive).~* Participant must have had a documented history of Lennox-Gastaut syndrome. This included written documentation of having met electroencephalogram (EEG) diagnostic criteria during the participant's history and evidence of at least 1 type of generalized seizure, including drop seizures (atonic, tonic, tonic-clonic or myoclonic) for at least 6 months.~* Participants had a history of slow ...

NCT02224560.name: GWPCARE3
NCT02224560.group: Lennox-Gastaut syndrome, cannabidiol 20 mg/kg/d vs placebo (wk 14, >=50% drop-seizure reduction RR)

allOutcomes.title: Proportion with >=50% reduction in drop seizure frequency (20 mg/kg arm) (RR)

NCT02224560.snippet: Source: Thiele EA et al. Lancet 2018;391:1085-1096 (GWPCARE3 LGS).

evidence.label: Enrollment
evidence.text: 225 patients (age 2-55) with Lennox-Gastaut syndrome and drug-resistant drop seizures randomized 1:1:1 double-blind to CBD 10 mg/kg/d (n=77), CBD 20 mg/kg/d (n=76), or placebo (n=73) over 14 weeks.

evidence.label: Primary - Median % Drop Seizure Reduction
evidence.text: Median % change in drop-seizure frequency: CBD 20 mg/kg -41.9% vs CBD 10 mg/kg -37.2% vs placebo -17.2%. Treatment difference 20 mg/kg vs placebo: -21.6 pp (95% CI -34.8 to -6.7; P=0.005). Primary met.

evidence.label: Secondary - Responder Rate, Safety
evidence.text: >=50% drop-seizure responder: CBD 20 mg/kg 40% (30/76) vs placebo 15% (11/73). Somnolence 14%, decreased appetite 9%, diarrhoea 12%. LFT abnormalities 25% of CBD patients on concomitant valproate.

evidence.label: Risk of Bias (RoB 2.0)
evidence.text: D1 LOW. D2 LOW - double-blind. D3 LOW - 95% completion. D4 LOW - central diary review. D5 LOW - SAP locked.

evidence.label: Eligibility (AACT-derived)
evidence.text: AACT-derived (auto-populated 2026-05-04). Sex: ALL; min age 2 Years; max age 55 Years; healthy volunteers: f. CRITERIA: "Key Inclusion Criteria:~* Participant and/or parent(s)/legal representatives were willing and able to give informed assent/consent for participation in the study.~* Participant and his or her caregivers were willing and able (in the investigator's opinion) to comply with all study requirements.~* Participant was male or female aged between 2 and 55 years (inclusive).~* Participant had a documented history of LGS. NCT02224703.name: GWPCARE4
NCT02224703.group: Lennox-Gastaut syndrome, cannabidiol 20 mg/kg/d vs placebo (wk 14, >=50% drop-seizure reduction RR)

allOutcomes.title: Proportion with >=50% reduction in drop seizure frequency (20 mg/kg arm) (RR)

NCT02224703.snippet: Source: Devinsky O et al. NEJM 2018;378:1888-1897 (GWPCARE4 LGS).

evidence.label: Enrollment
evidence.text: 171 patients with Lennox-Gastaut syndrome (age 2-55) and drug-resistant drop seizures randomized 1:1 double-blind to CBD 20 mg/kg/d (n=86) or matched placebo (n=85) for 14 weeks as adjunctive.

evidence.label: Primary - Median % Drop Seizure Reduction
evidence.text: Median % change in drop-seizure frequency: CBD 20 mg/kg -43.9% vs placebo -21.8%; treatment difference -17.2 pp (95% CI -30.3 to -4.1; P=0.01). Primary met.

evidence.label: Secondary - Responder Rate, OLE
evidence.text: >=50% drop-seizure responder: CBD 44% (38/86) vs placebo 24% (20/85). All-seizure reduction also favoured CBD. Somnolence 14%, decreased appetite 11%, LFT elevations typical with concomitant valproate.

evidence.label: Risk of Bias (RoB 2.0)
evidence.text: D1 LOW. D2 LOW - double-blind. D3 LOW - 91% completion. D4 LOW. D5 LOW - SAP locked.

evidence.label: Eligibility (AACT-derived)
evidence.text: AACT-derived (auto-populated 2026-05-04). Sex: ALL; min age 2 Years; max age 18 Years; healthy volunteers: f. CRITERIA: "Key Inclusion Criteria:~* Participant must have been male or female, aged between 2 and 18 years (inclusive).~* Participant must have had a documented history of Dravet syndrome that was not completely controlled by current antiepileptic drugs.~* Participant must have been taking 1 or more antiepileptic drugs at a dose that had been stable for at least 4 weeks.~* All medications or interventions for epilepsy (including ketogenic diet and vagus nerve stimulation) must have been stable for 4 week...

title: RapidMeta Neurology | Cannabidiol for Dravet and Lennox-Gastaut Syndrome v1.1
